patch.value:
## Algorithm portfolio

### Execution graph
Topological layering; longest weighted path and slack; dominators; prerequisite frontier; transitive reduction; minimum cut candidates; resource-constrained scheduling inputs.

### Impact graph
Reverse reachability; bounded personalized PageRank or diffusion; articulation and bridge analysis; fan-in or fan-out; dependency cones; affected evidence and ownership.

### Knowledge graph
Weak and strong components by kind family; Leiden or Louvain-style communities with deterministic seeds; degree, eigenvector, betweenness approximations; structural similarity; redundant and contradictory paths.

### Temporal and multi-agent graph
Incremental maintenance; snapshot deltas; edge survival and decay; change-point detection; event-time windows; merge or federation boundaries; contention hot spots.

### Context selection
Graph-distance features; spreading activation; prerequisite and evidence boosts; diversity-aware MMR or DPP-style packing; novelty and redundancy penalties; bounded anytime assembly.

All outputs must identify edge families, snapshot, exact or approximate status, limits, freshness, and why each result matters.
Graph analytics for planning and context: critical paths, blast radius, bottlenecks, communities, redundancy, and explainable relevance signals
Implement deterministic, incremental, and explainable algorithms that turn typed relationship data into planning decisions and token-efficient context signals.

Order-bearing DAG analysis provides topological order, critical path, slack, prerequisite depth, frontier, dominators, and transitive reduction while reporting genuine cycles separately; Impact analysis provides reverse reachability, bounded blast radius, articulation points or bridges, dependency fan-in and fan-out, bottlenecks, and change-risk propagation; Knowledge-graph analysis provides connected components, strongly connected components by edge family, community detection, centrality, redundancy, structural holes, and orphan classification; Algorithms are exact where bounded and expose deterministic approximation method, seed, error or confidence bounds, freshness, and omitted counts otherwise; Incremental and temporal variants consume immutable history and support snapshot comparison, trend, change-point, and counterfactual what-if analysis; Each signal includes provenance and a concise explanation suitable for context packing; no opaque score is authoritative by itself; Evaluation compares quality, latency, memory, update cost, and token savings on scratch, medium, adversarial, multi-decade, and one-million-item fixtures
Derive actionable project and context intelligence from typed graphs without conflating associative and order-bearing relationships
Agents and maintainers can find critical work, impact, bottlenecks, communities, missing links, and the best bounded context automatically
Improves prioritization, change safety, duplicate avoidance, context relevance, and portfolio understanding from tiny to million-item trackers
A library of snapshot-aware graph analytics with explanations, incremental updates, approximation bounds, and evaluation evidence
The context engine already plans multi-signal scoring but needs trustworthy graph semantics and scalable derived features

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Duplicate check 2026-07-13: context-engine items define relevance assembly and evaluation but no dedicated typed-graph analytics portfolio covering project planning,impact,community,and incremental temporal algorithms; this item supplies those graph primitives without duplicating ranking policy.
